gene_description: Cytochrome P450 1A2 — major hepatic enzyme responsible for metabolism of caffeine, clozapine, theophylline, olanzapine, and several other drugs. Activity is highly inducible by smoking, cruciferous vegetables, and chargrilled meat.

source.title: PharmGKB summary: very important pharmacogene information for CYP1A2
source.finding: CYP1A2*1F (rs762551 A allele) is associated with increased enzyme inducibility — AA homozygotes who smoke or consume inducers show ultrarapid caffeine metabolism and lower plasma levels of CYP1A2 substrates like clozapine and theophylline.

AA.effect: Homozygous *1F carriers show significantly increased CYP1A2 inducibility. In smokers or heavy coffee drinkers, enzyme activity is markedly elevated — rapid caffeine clearance, lower clozapine/olanzapine plasma levels, and faster theophylline metabolism. Without inducers, activity may be near-normal. Risk of sub-therapeutic clozapine levels if patient smokes.
AA.recommendation: For clozapine: smokers with AA genotype may need higher doses to achieve therapeutic levels. Monitor plasma concentrations closely. If patient quits smoking, reduce dose promptly to avoid toxicity. For caffeine: fast metabolizer — higher tolerance, may need more for effect. For theophylline: monitor levels; may need dose increase in smokers.

AC.effect: Heterozygotes show moderately increased CYP1A2 inducibility compared to CC wild-type. Intermediate caffeine metabolism. Clozapine and theophylline levels may be somewhat reduced in the presence of inducers (smoking, omeprazole).
AC.recommendation: Standard dosing for most CYP1A2 substrates. Monitor clozapine levels if patient is a smoker. Caffeine metabolism is moderate.

CA.effect: Heterozygotes show moderately increased CYP1A2 inducibility compared to CC wild-type. Intermediate caffeine metabolism. Clozapine and theophylline levels may be somewhat reduced in the presence of inducers (smoking, omeprazole).
CA.recommendation: Standard dosing for most CYP1A2 substrates. Monitor clozapine levels if patient is a smoker. Caffeine metabolism is moderate.

CC.effect: Wild-type CYP1A2 with standard inducibility. Normal caffeine metabolism — slower clearance compared to *1F carriers. Standard clozapine and theophylline metabolism. Less variation in drug levels with smoking status changes.
CC.recommendation: Standard dosing per prescribing guidelines. Slow caffeine metabolizer — may be more sensitive to caffeine effects, higher cardiovascular risk with high coffee intake (>3 cups/day).
